mnemonics: Hot Wallet: Online Like a Hot Potato (easily passed/traded, but also easily lost/stolen)
Cold Wallet: Offline Like a Cold Stone (secure, unmoving, and hard to access)
keyPoints: A wallet doesn't store crypto; it stores the private keys that prove ownership of crypto addresses on the blockchain.
Seed phrases (12-24 words) are a human-readable backup of a private key. Whoever has the seed phrase controls the wallet.
Wallets come in two main types: hot (connected to the internet, convenient) and cold (offline, more secure).
commonMistakes: Confusing a wallet address (public, for receiving funds) with a private key/seed phrase (secret, for spending funds).
Storing a seed phrase digitally (e.g., a screenshot, text file) which exposes it to hackers and malware.
Assuming an exchange account (e.g., Coinbase) is a personal wallet; you don't control the private keys, the exchange does.
